projectDescription: A Dead Simple VPN that tunnels traffic over TCP using modern, formally-verified cryptography with zero configuration files and minimal setup.
keyFeatures: Runs on TCP — works on restricted networks where only TCP/443 is open
Modern cryptography via the Charm library with formally verified implementations
Zero configuration files — single command to start server or client
~25 KB binary with no external runtime dependencies
Automatic firewall and routing rule management on Linux, macOS, OpenBSD, and BSDs
No heap allocations — small, constant memory footprint
Automatic reconnection on disconnect without traffic leaks
IPv6 leak prevention by blocking IPv6 on the client
BBR congestion control and bufferbloat mitigation via TCP_NOTSENT_LOWAT
publicApiSurface: dsvpn server <key file> [<server ip>|auto] [<port>|auto] [<tun iface>|auto] [<local tun ip>|auto] [<remote tun ip>|auto] [<external ip>|auto]
dsvpn client <key file> <server ip or name> [<port>|auto] [<tun iface>|auto] [<local tun ip>|auto] [<remote tun ip>|auto] [<gateway ip>|auto]
dd if=/dev/urandom of=vpn.key count=1 bs=32  — key generation
base64 < vpn.key  — export key as printable string
echo '<base64>' | base64 --decode > vpn.key  — import key from printable string
